User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the NeighborBuy application.

neighborhood.description: The neighborhood the user belongs to.

trustScore.description: User's reliability score based on community feedback.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a product or deal shared by a user.

userId.description: Reference to User who posted the product. (Relationship: User 1:N Product)

name.description: Name of the product or deal.

description.description: Detailed description of the product or deal.

category.description: Category of the product (e.g., food, household).

location.description: Geographic location of the product offer (e.g., street, complex).

ProductVerification.title: ProductVerification
ProductVerification.description: Represents user-submitted photos or videos verifying product details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the product verification entry.

productId.description: Reference to Product being verified. (Relationship: Product 1:N ProductVerification)

userId.description: Reference to User who submitted the verification. (Relationship: User 1:N ProductVerification)

mediaUrl.description: URL of the photo or video verification.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the verification was submitted.

Rating.title: Rating
Rating.description: Represents a user's rating and feedback on a seller's product.

raterId.description: Reference to User who gave the rating. (Relationship: User 1:N Rating as rater)

rateeId.description: Reference to User being rated (the seller). (Relationship: User 1:N Rating as ratee)

productId.description: Reference to Product being rated. (Relationship: Product 1:N Rating)

rating.description: Rating score given by the user (e.g., 1-5).

feedback.description: Textual feedback provided by the user.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the rating was submitted.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Access is limited to the user themselves.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores products created by a user. The userId path segment enforces ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

definition.description: Stores all products available on the marketplace. Includes a denormalized 'userId' field for efficient querying and authorization. Enables listing all products while still knowing the owner.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

definition.description: Stores product verifications. This leverages path-based ownership to ensure only users can create verifications for a product.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product.

params.name: verificationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product verification.

definition.description: Stores ratings given by a user. The userId path segment ensures only a user can create a rating.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: ratingId
params.description: The unique identifier of the rating.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the NeighborBuy application's core features while adhering to the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, and QAPs. It uses denormalization for authorization and structural segregation to simplify security rules.

*   `/users/{userId}`: Stores user profiles. This collection is straightforward and access is limited to the user themselves.
*   `/users/{userId}/products/{productId}`: Stores products created by a user. The `userId` path segment enforces ownership.
*   `/products/{productId}`: Stores all products available on the marketplace. This duplicates the data from `/users/{userId}/products/{productId}`, but enables efficient listing of all products without needing to query across multiple user subcollections (supports QAPs). Includes a denormalized `userId` field for efficient querying and authorization. This enables listing all products while still knowing the owner. Enables authorization rules to check if `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`.
*   `/products/{productId}/verifications/{verificationId}`: Stores product verifications. This leverages path-based ownership to ensure only users can create verifications for a product.
*   `/users/{userId}/ratings/{ratingId}`: Stores ratings given by a user.  The `userId` path segment ensures only a user can create a rating.

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization):**

*   The `products` collection denormalizes the `userId` from the user who created the product. This avoids the need for `get()` calls in security rules to verify ownership when listing or updating products.  Product verifications exist as a subcollection of products, eliminating the need to query other parts of the database.

**QAPs Support:**

*   The segregation of products into the top-level `/products` collection, with denormalized `userId` and other necessary metadata, supports efficient and secure `list` operations.  Rules can easily filter and authorize access based on these denormalized fields without needing complex queries or chained `get()` calls. Furthermore, segregating product verifications into their own subcollection enforces uniform security posture.
